mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 9.0 percent threshold, consumer spending collapses and recession fears dominate market pricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher equity valuations over a one-month horizon specifically during early recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early recovery periods, or the predictive power reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week trading horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon through rising wage-driven consumption.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in retail sales signal weakening consumer demand that precedes corporate earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The retail sales decline signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of late-cycle observations, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward testing.
